examples.title: Truth Table Generator for 2-Input Gates
examples.description: Program to generate truth tables for all basic 2-input logic gates
examples.explanation: This Python code implements all basic 2-input logic gates and generates a complete truth table showing outputs for all input combinations.

examples.title: C Implementation of Basic Logic Gates
examples.description: C program demonstrating implementation of AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR gates using bitwise operators
examples.explanation: This C program uses bitwise operators (&, |, ^, ~) to implement all basic logic gates and displays complete truth table.

examples.title: Half Adder Using XOR and AND Gates
examples.description: Algorithm showing implementation of half adder circuit using XOR and AND gates for binary addition
examples.explanation: Half adder adds two single bits. XOR produces sum (1+1=0 with carry 1), AND produces carry. This demonstrates practical XOR and AND gate usage.

examples.title: Majority Function Implementation
examples.description: Java program implementing majority function (output 1 when 2 or more of 3 inputs are 1)
examples.explanation: This Java code implements the majority function using Boolean algebra Y = AB + BC + AC. The gate implementation shows how AND gates feed into an OR gate to produce the final output.

examples.title: Verilog Module for 2-Input Logic Gates
examples.description: Verilog HDL code describing all basic 2-input logic gates
examples.explanation: This Verilog code shows how to describe basic logic gates in Hardware Description Language. Verilog is used for digital design and simulation.
